summary: McKenzie notices his Bingo Card Creator website is broken in Internet Explorer, with the layout appearing left-aligned instead of centered. The post is a brief, frustrated plea for help debugging a cross-browser CSS issue. He notes Firefox renders correctly while IE does not, despite the layout working fine previously. He asks readers using IE to check the site and report back.
key_points: Cross-browser compatibility between IE and Firefox was a major pain point for early web developers
McKenzie is learning web development on the fly while building his first software product
He leverages his blog audience as informal QA testers for his product
money_quotes: I am really starting to hate web development.
I just noticed that my page looks totally borked in IE — instead of being nice and centered its clumped at the left hand portion of the screen.
Firefox is, as usual, not effected.
key_insight: Building a real product forces you to confront the unglamorous realities of cross-browser web development, even when your core product has nothing to do with web technology.
